title: Malty Cheesy Jamaican Beef Stew
description: A one-pot Jamaican beef stew with a rich malty base and a vegan cheese pull, perfect for a comforting dinner.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat and brown the beef cubes on all sides.
Add chopped onion and minced garlic to the pot and sauté until the onion becomes translucent.
Stir in sliced carrot and chopped celery, cooking for another 5 minutes.
Add smoked paprika, tomato paste, and a splash of malt beer, stirring to coat the vegetables and meat.
Pour in beef broth, add bay leaf and thyme, bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer uncovered for 45 minutes or until the beef is tender.
Remove bay leaf, season with salt and pepper, and let the stew thicken slightly for 5 minutes.
In the last 10 minutes of cooking, sprinkle shredded vegan cheddar cheese over the stew and stir until it melts and creates a gooey pull.
Taste the stew and adjust seasonings if necessary.
Serve hot over steamed rice or with a side of crusty bread for a satisfying meal.
